reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
bond_broken: N-O bond (loss of water from nitrile oxide precursor)

reagents: ethyl nitroacetate (2.0 equiv) in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
feed_streams.reasoning: Both substrates are premixed in DES before heating. The nitrile oxide intermediate must be generated in the presence of the dipolarophile to avoid dimerization. No incompatibility between substrates at room temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ via thermal dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive reaction to completion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent system; acts as reaction medium and may promote nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent system

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to its aci-nitro (nitronic acid) form: ethyl nitroacetate → ethyl α-nitro enol
Dehydration of the nitronic acid form to generate the reactive nitrile oxide intermediate (ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide, EtOOC-CNO) in situ. The DES medium likely facilitates this elimination.
Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (1,3-dipole) with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively

stream_logic.reasoning: All components premixed in a single feed stream. Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate do not react at room temperature, so premixing is safe. The nitrile oxide is only generated upon heating in the reactor, and must be trapped immediately by the dipolarophile to prevent dimerization. A single stream ensures the dipolarophile is always present when nitrile oxide forms.

mixing_order_reasoning: Single stream is optimal. Both substrates are dissolved in the DES at room temperature where no reaction occurs. Upon entering the heated coil reactor, nitrile oxide is generated in situ and immediately trapped by phenylacetylene. Separate streams would risk a zone where nitrile oxide forms without dipolarophile present, leading to furoxan dimerization byproducts.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved; thermal pericyclic mechanism is unaffected by O2.
quench_reasoning: Reaction reaches completion in 15 min at 120°C. In flow, cooling the outlet stream to room temperature effectively stops the reaction. Batch protocol quenches with water for workup purposes only, not to stop a runaway reaction.
wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate followed by [3+2] cycloaddition is well-established. The DES viscosity (~50-100 cP at RT, lower at 120°C) may cause significant pressure drop in narrow-bore tubing — a 1.0 mm ID coil is recommended over smaller diameters. Back-pressure regulator (≥5 bar) advisable to prevent solvent boiling at 120°C (EG bp 197°C, but phenylacetylene bp 144°C). The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate accounts for competitive nitrile oxide dimerization; in flow with better mixing and heat transfer, it may be possible to reduce to 1.5 equiv. Residence time of 15 min is well-suited for flow. Single-stream approach is strongly preferred to ensure dipolarophile is always co-present with nascent nitrile oxide.
